analyst_observations: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 3 evals vs 63.1% baseline — a +37pp lift.
The skill's biggest structural wins are: explicit rating scale (0/3 baseline), distinct gaps section with severity labels (0/3 baseline), and conditional decision framework (1/3 baseline partial).

Bias detection is NOT a discriminating assertion — the baseline also flagged both biases in eval 3. Claude naturally picks up on geographic/credential proxy concerns. However, the with-skill version is more structured (dedicated Bias Check section with named bias types vs. embedded in prose).
French language adaptation works in both conditions — non-discriminating assertion.
Scoring rubric is mostly discriminating: with-skill always produces one (3/3), baseline produces one in 2/3 cases.
The with-skill version uses ~27% more tokens and takes ~44% longer — the cost of the additional structure (rating scale, gaps section, decision framework, structured questions with 4-part format).

Questions quality is strong in both conditions — Claude is naturally good at generating interview questions. The skill's value-add is the consistent 4-part structure (Why/Good/Probe/Red flag) rather than the question content itself.
